 {"id":"asupersync-1dl7s","title":"Sub-Epic: Database Stack - PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQLite with Cancel-Correct Semantics","description":"# Sub-Epic: Database Stack

## Overview
Implements first-party database clients with full Cx integration, structured
concurrency, and cancel-correct query semantics. Can raid parts from sqlmodel_rust.

## Background & Motivation
Tokio users will immediately say 'Postgres/MySQL/SQLite via sqlx.' To answer that,
Asupersync needs:
- Native async wire-protocol clients
- Connection pools with structured lifecycle
- Cancel-correct queries as obligations
- Deterministic DB testing story

## Raid Plan: sqlmodel_rust Parts

### Available Parts
| Crate | What to Raid | Target in asupersync |
|-------|--------------|---------------------|
| sqlmodel-postgres | PostgreSQL wire protocol, SCRAM auth | src/db/postgres/ |
| sqlmodel-mysql | MySQL wire protocol | src/db/mysql/ |
| sqlmodel-sqlite | SQLite bindings | src/db/sqlite/ |
| sqlmodel-pool | Connection pooling (budget-aware) | src/db/pool.rs |
| sqlmodel-core | Value/Row types, Error types | src/db/types.rs |

### Why Raid, Not Integrate
asupersync shouldn't depend on sqlmodel_rust. The wire protocols are the
valuable part - they work directly with asupersync's TcpStream.

## Cancel-Correct Query Semantics

### Query as Obligation
```rust
pub struct QueryHandle<'a> {
    conn: &'a mut Connection,
    state: QueryState,
}

impl<'a> QueryHandle<'a> {
    /// Execute and consume results.
    pub async fn fetch_all(self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Vec<Row>, DbError> {
        // Fulfills the obligation
    }
    
    /// Execute and get first row.
    pub async fn fetch_one(self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Row, DbError>;
    
    /// Execute and get optional row.
    pub async fn fetch_optional(self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Option<Row>, DbError>;
    
    /// Execute for side effects.
    pub async fn execute(self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<u64, DbError>;
}

impl<'a> Drop for QueryHandle<'a> {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if \!self.state.is_completed() {
            // Log warning: query not explicitly completed
            // Connection may need reset
        }
    }
}
```

### Connection Pool Lifecycle
- Pool = region-owned resource
- Request = scoped lease from pool
- close(pool) => quiescence (no in-flight queries)
- Cancellation drains server-side state

## Tasks in This Sub-Epic
1. PostgreSQL wire protocol client
2. MySQL wire protocol client
3. SQLite async wrapper
4. Connection pooling with budget integration
5. Database E2E tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PostgreSQL client with auth, queries, transactions
- [ ] MySQL client with auth, queries, transactions
- [ ] SQLite async wrapper
- [ ] Connection pooling with health checks
- [ ] Cancel-correct query semantics
- [ ] E2E tests with Docker
- [ ] Performance comparable to sqlx","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:30:07.109542256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:53.027738432Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:53.027625252Z","close_reason":"All children completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["database","ecosystem-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1dl7s","depends_on_id":"asupersync-l0jj8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1dlc6","title":"Re-add Label deriving (DecidableEq, Repr) in Lean","description":"Re-add `deriving` for `Label` (DecidableEq, Repr) in the Lean formalization so equality and representation are available for proofs, rewriting, and debugging. Update any dependent lemmas/instances that lost automation when the derives were removed. Preserve the semantics of `Label` and avoid introducing non-deterministic tactics.","acceptance_criteria":"1.   - label_repr_tick_stable

- cargo check --all-targets: pass
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: pass

Current blocker to closure:
- cd formal/lean && lake build fails on broader pre-existing Lean proof breakages outside this deriving slice (for example unknown identifier oid, multiple theorem proof failures around ~2189+, and recursive simp failures around ~3559+).
- This blocks acceptance criterion #1 (full Lean build passing) even though the deriving work itself is now present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyIsland","created_at":"2026-02-04T05:06:43.775701938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T04:43:16.554172181Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T04:43:16.554102932Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1dlc6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-zin5q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1523,"issue_id":"asupersync-1dlc6","author":"GentleHeron","text":"Reopened on 2026-02-15 by stall sweep: issue was in_progress with no updates since 2026-02-11; status reset to open for re-triage/claim.","created_at":"2026-02-15T03:35:28Z"},{"id":1530,"issue_id":"asupersync-1dlc6","author":"MistyIsland","text":"[2026-02-15 MistyIsland] Progress update: reproduced current Lean frontier using rch-offloaded execution and captured deterministic artifacts. Command used: rch exec -- /home/ubuntu/.elan/bin/lake -d /data/projects/asupersync/formal/lean build. Artifact log: target/lean-e2e/bd-2ikec_lake_build.log (325 lines). Extracted frontier via rch-offloaded conformance tool: rch exec -- cargo run --manifest-path conformance/Cargo.toml --bin lean_frontier_extract -- --log target/lean-e2e/bd-2ikec_lake_build.log --gap-plan formal/lean/coverage/gap_risk_sequencing_plan.json --out target/lean-e2e/bd-2ikec_frontier.json --source-log target/lean-e2e/bd-2ikec_lake_build.log. I am currently blocked from patching these lines due active exclusive reservation overlap on formal/lean/Asupersync.lean and formal/lean/coverage/* held by another active agent; coordination request sent in thread bd-2ikec.","created_at":"2026-02-15T03:50:17Z"},{"id":1535,"issue_id":"asupersync-1dlc6","author":"MistyIsland","text":"[2026-02-15 MistyIsland] Frontier reduction analysis on current bd-2ikec Lean failures (no file edits due active reservation by GentleHeron on formal/lean/Asupersync.lean).

From target/lean-e2e/bd-2ikec_lake_build.log, all 10 hard errors map to two local proof-shape patterns:
1) Asupersync.lean:2380 direction mismatch in setTask_same_region_preserves_wellformed_aux. Simplification produces h : newTask = task' but code expects task' = newTask.
2) Asupersync.lean {2521,2534,2547,2915,2928,2941,3015,3029,3043}: repeated Eq.trans orientation bug in commit/abort/leak branches. Current form uses (hOb'.trans hOb.symm), but hOb' already has shape some obStep = getObligation s o(0), so it should compose with hOb (not hOb.symm) to derive some obStep = some ob.

Patch intent:
- 2380 block: accept/symmetrize equality direction before substitution (e.g., derive newTask = task' then symm/subst, or derive task' = newTask via Eq.symm).
- All repeated trans sites: replace hOb'.trans hOb.symm with hOb'.trans hOb after relevant by_cases/subst alignment.

Expected impact: clears all 10 current errors in this frontier bucket family (proof-shape/application-type-mismatch + one type-direction mismatch).","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:00:10Z"},{"id":1544,"issue_id":"asupersync-1dlc6","author":"MistyIsland","text":"[2026-02-15 MistyIsland] Implemented and validated minimal proof-shape fixes in formal/lean/Asupersync.lean for the remaining bd-2ikec frontier blockers.

Code changes:

  - `have hTaskEq : task' = newTask := by simpa using h.symm`
- fixed 9 repeated Eq.trans orientation sites in committed/aborted/leaked obligation stability proofs:
  - replaced `hOb'.trans hOb` / prior broken forms with `hOb'.symm.trans hOb`
  - sites: 2521, 2534, 2547, 2915, 2928, 2941, 3015, 3029, 3043

 {"id":"asupersync-3qaym","title":"Phase 1+: Min-plus network calculus budgets (hard bounds)","description":"# Phase 1+: Min-Plus Network Calculus Budgets (Hard Bounds)

## Purpose
The design calls for upgrading scalar budgets (deadlines/quotas) into arrival/service curves in the min-plus semiring to provide provable backlog and latency bounds.

This is explicitly described as “required for hard bounds” and impacts:
- admission control
- backpressure
- buffer sizing

## Deliverables
- Curve representation types.
- min-plus convolution operations.
- integration points for scheduler/admission control.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Small demonstrator computing backlog/delay bounds for a simple pipeline.

 {"id":"asupersync-3qbhz","title":"Phase 3: Session Types (Optional, Advanced)","description":"# Phase 3: Session Types (Optional, Advanced)

## Purpose
Add an optional, opt-in layer of session-typed communication:
- encode protocol states in types
- prevent “forgot to ack/commit/abort” classes of bugs

## Scope
- Session type AST encoding (`Send`, `Recv`, `Choose`, `Offer`, `End`, etc.)
- Duality computation
- Typed endpoints integrated with two-phase channels
- Compile-time protocol compliance via typestate

## Constraints
- This is optional and may start as a separate crate/module.
- Must not compromise determinism.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Provides a session type representation + duality and a way to enforce protocol conformance.
- Integrates session-typed endpoints with two-phase/obligation-aware primitives.
- Compile-time compliance tests (e.g., trybuild or equivalent) are deterministic and explain failures clearly.

 {"id":"asupersync-3qdhk","title":"Implement Outcome type with severity lattice","description":"# Outcome Type with Severity Lattice

## Purpose
The Outcome type is the fundamental result type for all tasks and regions in Asupersync. Unlike Result<T, E>, it has FOUR variants ordered by severity, enabling monotone aggregation where "worse always wins."

## The Four Outcomes (Severity Order)
```rust
enum Outcome<V, E, R, P> {
    Ok(V),           // Severity 0: Success with value
    Err(E),          // Severity 1: Application error
    Cancelled(R),    // Severity 2: Cancelled with reason
    Panicked(P),     // Severity 3: Panic (unrecoverable)
}
```

## Why Four Values?
Traditional async runtimes conflate cancellation with errors or ignore it entirely. This causes:
- Silent data loss when cancellation drops in-progress work
- No distinction between "I failed" vs "I was stopped"
- Panics and errors treated the same way

Asupersync makes cancellation a first-class citizen because:
1. **Explicit Reasoning**: Code can pattern match on WHY something stopped
2. **Monotone Aggregation**: When combining outcomes, we want consistent behavior
3. **Policy-Aware**: Different outcomes may trigger different policies (restart, propagate, etc.)

## Mathematical Structure: Severity Lattice
The outcomes form a lattice under severity ordering:
```
Panicked
    ↑
Cancelled
    ↑
  Err
    ↑
  Ok
```

The lattice operations:
- **Join (⊔)**: Returns the more severe outcome (used for aggregation)
- **Meet (⊓)**: Returns the less severe outcome (rarely used)

## Key Operations

### severity() -> u8
Returns 0-3 for comparison. Must be implemented as a const fn for performance.

### combine(self, other) -> Self (for same V types)
Implements the join operation: `max_by_severity(self, other)`

This is used when:
- A region aggregates child outcomes
- A join combinator combines results
- Supervision decides on escalation

### is_terminal() -> bool
All variants are terminal (a task/region has finished).

### is_success() -> bool
Only Ok is success.

### into_result() -> Result<V, CombinedError<E, R, P>>
Converts to traditional Result for interop.

## Implementation Requirements

1. **Generic over all four type parameters**: V (value), E (error), R (cancel reason), P (panic payload)

2. **Default type parameters**:
   - E = Box<dyn Error + Send + Sync>
   - R = CancelReason
   - P = Box<dyn Any + Send>

3. **Must implement**:
   - Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq (when inner types do)
   - PartialOrd, Ord based on severity
   - From<Result<V, E>> for easy interop

4. **Combinators**:
   - map, map_err, map_cancelled, map_panicked
   - and_then, or_else
   - unwrap_or, unwrap_or_else
   - ok(), err(), cancelled(), panicked() extractors

## Invariant Preservation

This type supports INV-OBLIGATION-LINEAR: outcomes are absorbing states. Once a task reaches Completed(outcome), it cannot transition again.

## Testing Requirements

1. Severity ordering is correct: Ok < Err < Cancelled < Panicked
2. combine() always returns the more severe outcome
3. Lattice laws hold (associativity, commutativity, idempotence)
4. From<Result> conversions are correct

## Performance Considerations

- Outcome should be repr(u8) discriminant for fast severity checks
- No heap allocation for the Outcome enum itself
- Clone should be cheap (inner types may heap-allocate)

## Example Usage
```rust
let child_outcomes = vec![
    Outcome::Ok(42),
    Outcome::Err(MyError),
    Outcome::Ok(17),
];
let region_outcome = child_outcomes.into_iter()
    .reduce(|a, b| a.combine(b))
    .unwrap_or(Outcome::Ok(()));
// Result: Outcome::Err(MyError) - worst wins
```

## References
- asupersync_v4_formal_semantics.md §1.2 (Outcomes)
- asupersync_plan_v4.md §3.1 (Outcomes form a severity lattice)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Outcome has variants Ok/Err/Cancelled/Panicked with a total severity order (Ok < Err < Cancelled < Panicked).
- Provides `severity()` and ordering/aggregation helpers used by region close + combinators.
- Aggregation is monotone: combining outcomes never yields a "less severe" result.

 {"id":"asupersync-3qef7","title":"[FOUNDATION] Implement EvidenceLedger struct with serde serialization","description":"Implement the core EvidenceLedger struct in the franken_evidence crate with full serde serialization support. Fields: ts_unix_ms: u64, component: String, action: String, posterior: Vec<f64>, expected_loss_by_action: HashMap<String, f64>, chosen_expected_loss: f64, calibration_score: f64, fallback_active: bool, top_features: Vec<(String, f64)>. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: evidence_ledger::emit with component, action, trace_id. INFO: EvidenceLedger entry emitted (logged at the emission sink, not at struct construction).
